Microsoft Learn DAX documentation confirms MEDIAN function calculates median of numbers in a column; verified against https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/median-function-dax
Microsoft Learn DAX documentation confirms STDEV.P returns standard deviation of entire population; verified against https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dax/stdev-p-function-dax

Data analysis reveals limited categorical values: 5 unique Sales_Rep (Alice, Bob, Charlie, David, Eve), 4 unique Regions (East, North, South, West), 4 unique Categories (Clothing, Electronics, Food, Furniture); structured format with consistent decimal places indicates synthetic generation

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic sales data with 1000 records containing Sales_Amount values ranging from 100.12 to 9989.04. The median of Sales_Amount is 5019.30 and the population standard deviation is 2845.37. The task requires creating a histogram using binning with bin size 50, which would produce approximately 198 bins given the data range. Two DAX measures must be created: Median_Sales using the MEDIAN function and Stdev_Sales using the STDEV.P function for population standard deviation. The report must include a clustered column chart visual and a multi-row card visual, with a page renamed to 'Distribution Analysis'.
